Home/Services/Structured cabling

Structured cabling

Structured cabling for commercial buildings and business environments

Opticable installs the structured cabling that supports WiFi access points, cameras, access control, intercom, telephony, and the rest of the building network. The goal is a clean, organized, documented foundation that stays easier to support and expand.

Request a quoteView all services

Services

Cat 5e, Cat 6, Cat 6A, coaxial, patch panels, testing, and documentation for a clean building network base.

Structured cabling

What we install

  • Cat 5e, Cat 6, and Cat 6A copper cabling for workstations, WiFi access points, IP cameras, readers, and network equipment
  • Coaxial cabling for analog cameras, antennas, and existing signal distribution systems
  • Short-distance fiber optic links for connecting network rooms, floors, or remote zones
  • Patch panels for cable arrivals that stay organized, labeled, and ready for future growth

Practical use

What it supports in practice

  • PoE WiFi access points over Cat 6
  • IP cameras connected back to the NVR
  • Access-control readers wired to the control panel
  • IP phone handsets and office devices
  • Network equipment in the technical room

Plain language

What the main room, floor closet, and between-floor cabling do

The main network room is where the core equipment and handoffs usually live. A floor closet or secondary cabinet is a smaller distribution point closer to the spaces being served. The cabling between floors links those points together so devices do not all have to run back in an improvised way.

Quality

What a clean installation actually means

Cables labeled at both ends, pathways secured properly, organized patch panels, and a network room that any technician can understand without rediscovering everything from scratch.

Process

How the work is handled

  • 01 - Site survey: areas to cable, available pathways, and building constraints.
  • 02 - Cabling plan: number of points, run lengths, cable type, and network-room organization.
  • 03 - Structured installation: cable pulling, patch-panel termination, and full identification of every point.
  • 04 - Testing and certification: every point is tested and the point list is handed over.

Building types

Where this service is most often used

These pages show the building types where this service is commonly part of the project.

Technology for office buildings

Entrances to secure, technical rooms to control, uneven WiFi coverage, network rooms to clean up, and new workstations or tenants to connect.

View this industry

Technology for multi-tenant buildings

Intercom or video intercom, access control for entrances, cameras in common areas, shared WiFi, and a simple building-management workflow.

View this industry

Technology for retail and sales floors

Cameras at entrances and cash areas, stable WiFi for operations, controlled access to back areas, and clean cabling for point-of-sale systems.

View this industry

Technology for warehouses and light industrial sites

Cameras around docks and perimeter, controlled access for sensitive zones, WiFi for operations, and stronger backbone links between technical areas.

View this industry
View all industries

Multifamily buildings

Pages tied to multifamily WiFi and network foundations

These pages show how this service fits multifamily buildings at different building sizes.

WiFi and network infrastructure for multifamily buildings

When that foundation is planned properly, WiFi, cameras, intercom, access control, and the other building systems stay more stable, easier to expand, and simpler to manage together.

View this page

WiFi and network infrastructure for 10 to 25 units

A readable main network room, clean cable paths, well-placed WiFi access points, and a realistic scope for the common areas.

View this page

Network infrastructure for 25 to 100 units

A main network room, floor cabinets or closets, cleaner vertical links, and a real distribution logic between the building zones become much more common.

View this page

WiFi and infrastructure for 100+ unit buildings

The larger the building, the earlier the main room, floor distribution, fiber, common areas, entry systems, and operating logic need to be made clear.

View this page

Useful guides

Helpful pages before your structured cabling request

These pages answer common planning, pricing, and decision questions before a site visit or quote.

Structured cablingNetwork dropsPricing

How much does structured cabling cost in an office or retail space?

Structured cabling pricing depends much more on paths, drops, rack condition, testing, and finish quality than on cable alone.

Reading time3 min read

Cat6AFiber opticCommercial cabling

Cat6A or fiber: which makes sense for your commercial building?

Cat6A and fiber are not interchangeable. The right choice depends on distance, capacity, equipment rooms, and the role of the cable.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedFebruary 11, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
Network roomRack cleanupInfrastructure

When should you clean up the network room or rack?

Rack cleanup makes future camera, WiFi, access control, and cabling work easier to support and safer to modify.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedMarch 5, 2026
Reading time3 min read

Request a quote

Do you have a cabling project or a network room that needs to be organized? Tell us the square footage, point count, and building type.

Opticable serves commercial buildings in Montreal, Laval, Longueuil, the South Shore, the North Shore, the Laurentians, and across Quebec.

Services

Related services

Related services that often support the same technology roadmap.

Fiber optic

Backbone links between floors, network rooms, and distant zones when capacity or distance outgrows copper.

View service

Network infrastructure

Main network rooms, racks, patch panels, handoffs, and clean distribution for building systems that are easier to operate.

View service

Commercial WiFi

WiFi quality, access points, coverage planning, cabling, and network foundations for commercial buildings.

View service

Security cameras

IP cameras, PoE, NVRs, remote viewing, and ongoing support to protect sensitive areas, common spaces, perimeters, and entries.

View service

Access control

Readers, locks, panels, door cabling, and ongoing access administration for lobbies, entries, and restricted areas.

View service

Intercom

Audio or video intercom systems for lobbies, gates, and visitor entries, integrated with access control and the network.

View service

IT services

Device management, network support, maintenance, and technical support for your environment after installation.

View service

IP telephony

Business telephony, SIP lines, handsets, VoIP cabling, and network support for commercial offices.

View service

Opticable installs and supports cameras, access, WiFi, intercom, cabling, and network infrastructure for commercial buildings in Montreal, Laval, Longueuil, and across Quebec.
